Loomis Gang.description: The Loomis Gang was a family of outlaws operating in Central New York during the mid-19th century, founded by George Washington Loomis Sr. and his wife Rhoda Mallet. The gang engaged in horse theft, livestock rustling, burglary, and counterfeiting, expanding into a multi-state syndicate under Wash Loomis. They cultivated local goodwill to avoid prosecution and were active from the early 1800s until their decline in the 1860s.

La Familia Michoacana.description: Mexican criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking and other illegal activities, designated as a foreign terrorist organization by the U.S. State Department. Founded in the 1980s as a vigilante group in Michoacán, it later allied with the Gulf Cartel. In 2011, it split into two factions, with one retaining the name La Familia Michoacana and the other becoming Los Caballeros Templarios. The group was largely expelled from Michoacán and retreated to the State of Mexico and Guerrero. Its main leader, Nazario Moreno González, was killed in 2014.

Organised crime in Pakistan.description: The Pakistani mafia is a network of ethnically based criminal organizations involved in drug trafficking, assassination, land grabbing, arms smuggling, and other illegal activities. It operates primarily in Pakistan but has international reach, including in the United Kingdom and Scandinavia. The group has alleged ties to the Indian underworld figure Dawood Ibrahim and his D-Company, as well as to Pakistan's Inter-Services Intelligence. Notable gangs include the Chotu gang, which was dismantled in a military operation in 2023.

'Ndrina Muto.description: The 'Ndrina Muto is a 'ndrina (criminal family) of the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria, based in Cetraro. It is involved in illegal activities in the tourism sector, the fish market, drug trafficking, and has connections in the Cilento area of Campania. The group was allied with the Pino-Sena in a feud against the Perna-Pranno-Vitelli in the late 1970s-1980s, resulting in 27 deaths. It has also been implicated in the sinking of radioactive waste ships.

Miami Boys.description: The Miami Boys was a drug trafficking organization based in Liberty City, Miami, Florida, founded in 1982 by Issac Hicks and James Sawyer. It was structured like organized crime with ranks and territorial quadrants, using weapons like AK-47s and Uzis. By 1989, it expanded to cities across the South and Midwest, including Atlanta, Tampa, Jacksonville, Augusta, and Columbus, Ohio. The gang was largely eliminated in Atlanta in 1996 and the last member in Jacksonville was arrested in 1999.

Mungiki.description: Mungiki is a banned ethnic organization in Kenya, originating in the late 1980s. It promotes a return to traditional Kikuyu customs and African spirituality, rejecting Westernization and Christianity. The group has been involved in ethnic violence, anti-government resistance, and criminal activities such as protection racketeering. It is secretive and has a cell structure, with alleged membership of up to 500,000 at its peak.

'Ndrina Caligiuri.description: The Caligiuri are a 'ndrina of the Calabrian 'Ndrangheta originating from Bagnara Calabra, operating in Petronà and Cirò Marina, with connections in Lazio, Lombardy, Germany, and the United States (Brooklyn, New York) with the Colombo and Gambino families. They are allied with the Farao-Marincola and Coco-Trovato clans. In the 1970s, a family feud against the Santoro of Cirò Marina began, leading to multiple murders and arrests for heroin trafficking.

116th Street Crew.description: The 116th Street Crew is a faction of the Genovese crime family based in East Harlem, Manhattan, New York City. Founded in the 1890s by the Morello crime family, it was originally known as the 116th Street Mob. Under caporegime Anthony Salerno in the 1960s, the crew operated from the Palma Boys Social Club. It absorbed members of the East Harlem Purple Gang in the late 1970s and early 1980s. The crew remains active in racketeering, murder, extortion, illegal gambling, loansharking, and bookmaking.
